title: Urceolina robledoana
taxonomy: Urceolina robledoana is classified within the family Amaryllidaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Amaryllidaceae family. The genus Urceolina is a group of flowering plants that are native to South America, and Urceolina robledoana is one of the species within this genus. intro: Urceolina robledoana, also known as Urceolina, is a plant species in the family Amaryllidaceae, which is native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it a potential treatment for pain relief, muscle spasms, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Urceolina include alkaloids, such as urceoline and robledoamine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Urceolina is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
